messages.content:
Which of the following should be included during the planning of personnel requirements?

Employee parking

Process design

Product design

All other choices.
Process design and All other choices. Employee parking and product design are not directly related to the planning of personnel requirements.

Who of the following is mainly responsible for determining HOW the product is to be produced?

Product designer

Business owner

Process designer

Schedule designer
Process designer

Which of the following is NOT part of the facilities design component of the facilities planning?

Handling system design

Facility systems design

Facilities location

Layout design
Facilities location. It is part of the facilities location component of facilities planning which is separate from facilities design.

Why would a bill of materials be helpful in designing a facility?

Lists all the parts required to produce a product

Help in determining the storage requirements at each station

Help in determining the size of the receiving area

All other choices

All other choices. A bill of materials would be helpful in designing a facility because it will help in determining the storage requirements at each station, the size of the receiving area, and all the parts required to produce a product.

Which of the following is a Layout Design Consideration?

Higher utilization of space, equipment, and people

Improved flow of information, materials, or people

Flexibility

All other choices
All other choices. Higher utilization of space, equipment, and people, improved flow of information, materials, or people, and flexibility are all layout design considerations.

Which of the following is a factor that can affect the location selection?

Proximity to markets

Proximity to raw materials and suppliers

Labor costs

All other choices
All other choices. Proximity to markets, proximity to raw materials and suppliers, and labor costs are all factors that can affect the location selection.

Monte Carlo simulation can be used when a the model is …. and process input is ….

Dynamic and Stochastic

Static and Stochastic

Dynamic and Deterministic

Static and Deterministic
Dynamic and Stochastic. Monte Carlo simulation is often used when the model is dynamic (i.e., changes over time) and stochastic (i.e., involves random variables or uncertainties) in nature.

Set of related components working together toward some purpose, usually over time” is the definition of …

Process

Event

System

Object
System. A system is a set of related components working together toward some purpose, usually over time.

a document that is created by simulationist to describe exactly what will be delivered, when, how, and by whom” is the definition of …

Stakeholder

Functional specification

Word document

Object
Functional specification.

If customers arrival randomly in a queueing system, the arrival process in this system is described as …

Discrete process

Continuous process

Stochastic process

Deterministic process

Stochastic process. The customers arrive randomly over time, which makes it a stochastic process. Deterministic processes are predictable, while stochastic processes are not, which is the case with a queueing system where customers arrive randomly.

Decision making under certainty has.

only one state of nature

more than one state of nature

unknown state of nature

one or more than one state of nature
Only one state of nature. Decision making under certainty assumes that there is only one state of nature, and the outcome of each alternative is known with certainty.

The level of risk is high in decision making system which has.

Low mean

High mean

Low std. deviation

High std.Deviation

High standard deviation. The level of risk in decision-making system which has a high standard deviation is high because the possible outcomes of the decision have a large variation around the expected value. This means that there is a higher level of uncertainty and unpredictability associated with the decision. Low mean and high mean refer to the expected value of the decision outcome, while low standard deviation implies less variation in the outcome and hence less risk.

The randomness of people, parts or machine in manufacturing industry which make decision hard is also known as:

Uncertainty

Variability

Complexity

Sensitivity

Variability. The randomness of people, parts, or machines in the manufacturing industry that makes decision making hard is known as variability. It refers to the variations that occur in the manufacturing process due to differences in the input materials, machinery, or human performance. These variations can be random and difficult to predict, making it hard to make optimal decisions that maximize efficiency and quality. Uncertainty refers to the lack of knowledge or information about the future outcomes of a decision, while complexity refers to the nature of the decision problem itself and the number of factors involved. Sensitivity refers to how much a decision outcome changes with small variations in the input parameters.

The probability of going from current state to same state is always.

1

0

0.5

0-1

1. The probability of going from the current state to the same state is always one in a Markov Process or Markov Chain. This is because the Markov property, which states that the future state of a system depends only on the current state, not on the past states. Since the current state is known, the probability of staying in the same state is always one.

How many experiment runs does a full factorial design with 3 factors, each factor at 4 levels, consist of?

64

81

12

7

The experiment runs in a full factorial design with 3 factors, each factor at 4 levels, can be calculated by multiplying the number of levels of each factor together. Thus, the total number of runs would be 4 x 4 x 4 = 64. Therefore, a full factorial design with 3 factors, each factor at 4 levels, consists of 64 experiment runs. Answer: 64.

In Design of Experiments, performing the same treatment combination more than one as a new experiment is called

Repetition.

Replication.

Randomization.

Blocking.

Replication. In Design of Experiments, performing the same treatment combination more than once as a new experiment is called replication. Replication helps to estimate the experimental error and improves the precision and accuracy of the results. Replication is different from repetition, which involves repeating the entire experiment with a new random sample of experimental units. Randomization refers to the random assignment of experimental units to treatment combinations to reduce bias and increase the generalizability of the results. Blocking refers to the grouping of experimental units that are similar in some way so that they are assigned to each treatment combination in equal proportions, thus reducing the variability in the response.

ANOVA is a statistical test of comparing three or more

population means.

sample means.

population standards deviation.

sample standards deviation.

Population means. ANOVA (Analysis of Variance) is a statistical test used to compare the means of three or more populations. It helps to determine whether there are statistically significant differences between the means of the populations. ANOVA can be used to compare sample means as well, but its primary purpose is to compare population means. ANOVA can also be used to test for differences in variances or standard deviations, but this is usually done in the context of comparing the means of two or more groups, rather than evaluating a single group.

A researcher recruits 80 students for a study. Half of the students attend a review session for an exam. The remaining students study as they normally would for the exam. After the exam, the researcher compares the scores of the two groups. To analyze the obtained results, the research should be use:

One-way ANOVA

Two-way ANOVA

Paired t-test

two-samples t-test

Two-samples t-test. The researcher recruited two independent samples (the students who attended the review session and those who didn't) and compared their exam scores. Therefore, the appropriate statistical test to analyze the results is a two-sample t-test. One-way ANOVA and two-way ANOVA are used when there are three or more groups being compared, while the paired t-test is used when the data is paired or matched (e.g., before-and-after measurements on the same individuals). However, in this case, the two groups are independent and not paired, so the paired t-test would not be appropriate.

The assumption used in ANOVA is

The population from which the samples were obtained must be normally distributed.

The samples must be independent.

The variances of the population must be equal.

All other choices.

All other choices. In addition to the mentioned assumptions, ANOVA also assumes that the data is continuous (i.e., measured on an interval or ratio scale), the groups being compared have approximately equal sample sizes, and the outcomes are independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.) within and between groups. Additionally, ANOVA assumes that the residuals (i.e., differences between the observed and predicted values) are normally distributed with a mean of zero and constant variance across all groups. This assumption can be checked using diagnostic plots or statistical tests, such as the Shapiro-Wilk test or the Q-Q plot.

In one-way ANOVA, with usual notation (n: number of experiments, k number of levels), the degree of freedom of the error is equal to:

(n – 1

(k – 1)

(n – k – 1)

(n – k)

(n – k). In one-way ANOVA, the degree of freedom of the error (or residual degrees of freedom) is equal to (n - k), where n is the total number of observations and k is the number of levels or groups being compared. This represents the total number of observations minus the total number of groups or levels. The degrees of freedom of the between-group (or treatment) variation is k-1, which represents the number of independent groups minus one. The total degrees of freedom is equal to (n-1), which is the total number of observations minus one, and represents the total variability in the data that can be partitioned into between-group and within-group variation.
